figure.title: PCS Block Diagram - Variant 1
figure.purpose: Shows the high-level transmit and receive processing steps within the Physical Coding Sublayer (PCS).

technical_summary: Transmit path (Tx): Data from the Reconciliation Sub Layer undergoes Scrambling, Transcoding, Alignment Overwrite, FEC Encoding, and Symbol Distribution before reaching the PMA Sub Layer.
Receive path (Rx): Data from the PMA undergoes Alignment lock and deskew, Lane reorder, FEC Decoding, Alignment Overwrite, Transcoding, and Descrambling before passing up.
Notably in this variant, Scramble occurs before Transcode on the Tx path.

components.name: PCS
components.description: Physical Coding Sublayer, handles encoding, scrambling, and forward error correction.

components.name: Reconciliation Sub Layer
components.description: Interface to the upper layer (Data Link).

ordered_flow: Scramble -> Transcode -> Alignment Overwrite -> FEC Encode -> Symbol Distribution

critical_facts: The PCS abstracts the physical medium by providing encoding, scrambling, and FEC.
